hooks.prompt: This command is about to run a git commit. If it creates a commit message (-m or -F or heredoc), verify the message subject follows Conventional Commits: <type>(<optional scope>): <subject>, where type is one of feat, fix, chore, refactor, test, docs, style, perf, build, ci, revert. Fail with a reason naming the correct format if it does not. Pass if the command does not set a message (e.g. --amend --no-edit, merge continuation). $ARGUMENTS

statusLine.command: bash -c 'input=$(cat); cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".workspace.current_dir"); dir=$(basename "$(dirname "$cwd")")/$(basename "$cwd"); remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".context_window.context_left_until_compact_percentage // empty"); if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then ctx_info=$(printf "\033[0;33m[ctx:%d%%]\033[0m " "${remaining%.*}"); else ctx_info=""; fi; if git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir >/dev/null 2>&1; then branch=$(git -C "$cwd" -c core.useBuiltinFSMonitor=false -c core.fsmonitor=false symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "detached"); if ! git -C "$cwd" -c core.useBuiltinFSMonitor=false -c core.fsmonitor=false diff --quiet 2>/dev/null || ! git -C "$cwd" -c core.useBuiltinFSMonitor=false -c core.fsmonitor=false diff --cached --quiet 2>/dev/null; then status=" ✗"; else status=""; fi; git_info=$(printf " \033[1;34mgit:(\033[0;31m%s\033[1;34m)%s\033[0m" "$branch" "$status"); else git_info=""; fi; printf "%s\033[1;32m➜\033[0m  \033[0;36m%s\033[0m%s" "$ctx_info" "$dir" "$git_info"'
